paper_title: NanoSIMS and Isotopic Analysis of Phytoplankton and Bacteria

methods.description: This method involves the use of nanoSIMS to analyze isotopic compositions of phytoplankton and bacteria cells. It is used to determine the isotopic ratios of carbon and nitrogen in cells, which helps in understanding cellular processes and interactions.

steps.description: Additional cuts from the same polycarbonate filters used for the previous CARD-FISH analysis were collected.
steps.searchable_summary: collect additional filter cuts for nanoSIMS analysis

steps.description: An additional CARD-FISH analysis was performed to locate the Alteromonas and Pseudoalteromonas cells for nanoSIMS analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform CARD-FISH analysis to locate specific cells

steps.description: Filters containing 15N and 13C labelled cells were hybridized using HRP-labeled probes and amplified with fluorine containing fluorescently labelled tyramides.
Diatom cells and hybridized bacteria were mapped using a laser microdissection system.
The LMD marked areas were analyzed using a nanoSIMS 50 L with a primary Cs+ ion beam.
Sample surface was treated with low-energy Cs+ beam before analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: treat sample surface with Cs+ beam

steps.description: Secondary ions were analyzed for their mass to charge ratio and detected using collectors.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze secondary ions for m/z ratio

steps.description: Scans 3-8 were considered for analysis using LANS software for isotopic ratio analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze scans for isotopic ratios using LANS

steps.description: Regions of interest (ROIs) were drawn inside the cells to determine cell-specific isotope ratios.
steps.searchable_summary: draw ROIs for cell-specific isotope ratios

methods.description: This method involves presenting the isotopic composition data and performing statistical analyses to correct for isotopic dilution effects.

steps.description: The C and N cellular isotope composition measured by nanoSIMS is presented as fractional isotope abundances (atom%).
steps.searchable_summary: present isotope composition as fractional abundances

steps.description: Correct for isotopic dilution by analyzing cells with and without CARD-FISH treatment.
steps.searchable_summary: correct isotopic dilution using CARD-FISH data
inputs: isotope data with and without CARD-FISH

steps.description: Calculate the proportion of DDC and DDN in bacteria at each time point.
